title: Marubeni and HIS Collaborate to Reduce CO2 Emissions from Air Travel:  Launch of a Tour in Spain Utilizing SAFâ€™s CO2 Reduction Attributes
content:
Marubeni Corporation has sold indirect COâ‚‚ reduction attributesâ€”generated through the use of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF)â€”to H.I.S. Co., Ltd. (HIS) via a certificate-based system under the Book and Claim mechanism. This marks the first instance of a travel agency using such a program to reduce the environmental footprint of a tour package.

HIS will incorporate these attributes into a planned 8-day Spain tour departing from Narita Airport on Iberia Airlines flights scheduled between November 2025 and January 2026. By doing so, they will effectively reduce up to two-thirds of the COâ‚‚ emissions typically associated with aircraft use for round-trip economy class travel.

The aviation sector is responsible for around 2% of global COâ‚‚ emissions, and demand is growing. The IATA aims for net-zero emissions by 2050, with SAF adoption playing a pivotal role. SAF can cut COâ‚‚ emissions by up to 80% over its lifecycle, and is typically derived from used cooking oil or animal fats.

Marubeniâ€™s certificate program, verified by ClassNK, ensures transparency in the accounting and allocation of COâ‚‚ reductions. HIS, which is committed to achieving net-zero emissions across its entire supply chain by 2050, views this initiative as part of its broader strategy involving electric vehicles, SAF investment, and sustainable tourism.

Both companies plan to expand the initiative to further support aviation decarbonization and raise awareness among travelers of the climate impact of flying.
author: Analysts Account
